actionable_feedback: Add an explicit deterministic rounding policy to the memo: define whether residual expected hits are rounded up, down, or thresholded to zero, and show the rounding rule applied per-query and in aggregate (include rationale and legal/privacy escalation criteria).

Make spreadsheet cell-level formulas inspectable: ensure formulas are present in workbook cells (not only cached computed values) and re-save the .xlsx so tools like openpyxl can parse them. If OOXML writer was used, include a short note explaining where formulas are placed and provide a CSV or a Python script (with the same formulas) to reproduce calculations programmatically.

Explicitly state mitigation-evaluation assumptions in the memo: declare whether mitigations were evaluated as separate, independent scenarios (as currently done) or applied sequentially/combined, and include the scenario matrix used.

Provide deterministic integer-enforcement scenarios: attach additional workbook tabs or a script that applies the declared rounding policy (e.g., floor/ceil/nearest) and shows whether any rounding policy would change the gate result (pass vs fail).
Document reproducibility steps: add a one-paragraph 'How to re-run' that lists required tools (e.g., openpyxl or included Python script) and the steps to reproduce numbers from the embedded packet.
artifact_specific_comments: privacy_gate_memo.md: Clear, well-structured memo that documents packet values, mitigation rates, formulas, row-level calculations, sensitivity analysis, and conservative decision rationale.

vector_pii_suppression.xlsx: Workbook exists and contains computed values; grader trace indicates workbook formulas were cached into OOXML but cell-level formulas could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable). Include live formulas or a companion script/CSV to allow automated verification.
feedback_summary: Submission produced required deliverables, applied mitigation math correctly, included row-level citations and sensitivity analysis, and provided a defensible conservative decision (hold). Minor gaps: (1) no explicit deterministic rounding/operational policy for fractional residual hits, and (2) the grader could not programmatically verify live spreadsheet cell formulas (formulas appear cached in OOXML). Addressing these will remove the small deduction and improve reviewer reproducibility.

limitations_documented: Spreadsheet (.xlsx) cell-level formulas could not be programmatically parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable)

trace_specific_comments: Trace shows an attempt to use the bundled @oai/artifact-tool and a Node runtime import failed due to a native binary code-signature mismatch; the author fell back to manual OOXML writing. Note this fallback in the memo and include instructions for reviewers to parse formulas.

The trace documents that workbook formulas were cached into the OOXML package; confirm and surface the specific OOXML path or include an alternative reproducible calculation artifact (e.g., Python script).
